response.justification: Bogdan Szabo has a broad software background with genuine geospatial exposure (GISCollective, Green Map GIS, FOSS4G talk) and distributed systems experience at scale (Dunnhumby, millions of req/s microservices), but his primary toolchain is firmly TypeScript/Node.js. His C++ experience is limited to a short stint at National Instruments in 2014-2015 and a bachelor thesis project — neither modern nor at the depth required for production routing pipeline work. There is no Rust experience, no navigation or routing systems work, and no evidence of geospatial data pipeline engineering at the tile/graph level this role demands.
strengths: Hands-on geospatial/GIS project experience including a production open-source geo-data platform and FOSS4G conference talk
Proven exposure to high-throughput distributed systems (low-latency DLang services handling millions of HTTP req/s at Dunnhumby)
Strong craftsmanship culture — TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development, and a track record of quality-focused engineering across many environments
concerns: No modern C++ or Rust expertise; primary language stack is TypeScript/JavaScript/Node.js with only dated, surface-level C++ history
Geospatial work is web-app GIS (point collection, map display), not routing graph construction, tile generation, or navigation pipeline engineering
No evidence of experience with routing algorithms, map data transformation, traffic signal integration, or navigation QA processes
